---
title: "WDI 2022 Snapshot: Growth, Inflation, and Health"
author: "Hank Standaert"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Overview

This report analyzes a 2022 sample of the World Development Indicators (WDI) dataset for 217 countries.  
Data source: World Bank WDI [@worldbank_wdi]. Data was downloaded with `wbgapi` [@wbgapi].

## Load data

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("wdi.csv")
df.head()
```

```{python}
df.shape
```

```{python}
df["gdp_per_capita"].describe()
```

# GDP per capita distribution

```{python}
#| label: fig-gdp-hist
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of GDP per capita (current US$), 2022. Source: World Bank WDI."
plt.figure()
df["gdp_per_capita"].dropna().plot(kind="hist", bins=30)
plt.title("GDP per capita distribution (2022)")
plt.xlabel("GDP per capita (current US$)")
plt.ylabel("Number of countries")
plt.show()
```

# Inflation vs GDP growth (scatter)

```{pyhton}
#| label: fig-inflation-growth
#| fig-cap: "Inflation rate vs GDP growth rate (2022). Source: World Bank WDI."
sub = df[["inflation_rate", "gdp_growth_rate"]].dropna()

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(sub["inflation_rate"], sub["gdp_growth_rate"])
plt.title("Inflation vs GDP growth (2022)")
plt.xlabel("Inflation rate (%)")
plt.ylabel("GDP growth rate (%)")
plt.show()
```

# Top 10 countries by life expectancy

```{python}
top10 = df[["country","life_expectancy"]].dropna().sort_values("life_expectancy", ascending=False).head(10)
top10
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-life-top10
#| fig-cap: "Top 10 countries by life expectancy (2022). Source: World Bank WDI."
plt.figure()
plt.barh(top10["country"], top10["life_expectancy"])
plt.title("Top 10 life expectancy (2022)")
plt.xlabel("Life expectancy (years)")
plt.ylabel("Country")
plt.gca().invert_yaxis()
plt.show()
```

# Key statistics table

```{python}
stats = df[["gdp_per_capita","inflation_rate","gdp_growth_rate","life_expectancy"]].describe().T
stats = stats[["count","mean","std","min","50%","max"]]
stats
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Summary statistics for selected indicators (2022). Source: World Bank WDI."
stats.round(2)
```

# Notes on missing data

```{python}
missing = df.isna().mean().sort_values(ascending=False)
missing.head(10)
```
